_comment: Post-uplift captures for the Draw.io Quality Uplift programme.
Updated as each scenario (G1..G7) is recaptured through the agent

g1-three-tier-web.observations: Strict retries=0 (1 add-cells batch with 17 cells, 0 clear-diagram, 0 corrective edits). Friction events: (1) 3 XML-extraction round-trips between add-cells and finish-diagram (was 4 in baseline; agent still uses temp files for >50KB payload — acceptable per agent contract); (2) heavy front-loaded skill-reference reading (10 read/search calls before first MCP call) as the agent loads the new references for diagram-types, semantic-zones, legend-template, icon-variants. Improvements: ONE search-shapes (was 2; T-035 contract honored), ONE finish-diagram (was 2; T-036 honored), NO post-export palette correction (was 1 sed call; T-027 presets working). Cost: 0 + 2 = 2 (vs baseline 3 — 33% reduction). Source: Copilot Chat session 3253ce4f-621e-4771-a83d-1a1fff84f77f.
quality_issues: BREAKTHROUGH: rubric mean 3.29/4 — FIRST G1 above the 3/4 acceptance bar (+0.43 vs baseline 2.86/4). All three layout/semantics/labelling dimensions improved by 1 point each.
WIN (semantics +1): Trust boundary now rendered as an actual cell (was only mentioned in legend before — pure regression-fix from T-019/T-008 work).
WIN (semantics +1): Observability zone container with App Insights + Log Analytics inside (was floating cross-cutting in baseline — T-019 + T-022).
WIN (labelling +1): Legend cell present with edge-style key (HTTPS / SQL / Telemetry) — was missing entirely in baseline (T-021 + T-022).
WIN (workflow): zero post-export sed/regex palette correction (T-027 apex-arch presets), one search-shapes call (T-035 contract), one finish-diagram (T-036).

VISIBLE REGRESSION: diagram title at top (e.g., 'Three-Tier Web Application — Sweden Central') is missing from the recapture but was present in baseline. Recommend a small skill-side reinforcement to ensure title-bar rendering survives the new contract.

PERSISTING ISSUE: edge-label collision near Web App→SQL and Web App→Storage (SQL/HTTPS/HTTPS stacked). Same root cause as baseline; T-006 validator catches it. T-025 auto-arrange (deferred) is the proper fix.
MINOR: Storage → Key Vault edge invented by agent (not in prompt; prompt says Web App accesses Key Vault for secrets). Semantic-target correctness is out of scope for Wave 4 deliveries.
MINOR: App Service Plan rendered as separate icon (prompt listed it; abstraction-rules.md says it's hosting detail). Agent took the prompt literally; not strictly wrong.
MINOR: Trust boundary scoping wraps only Web App label area rather than the architecture perimeter. Visually noisy but better than baseline (which rendered nothing).

g2-hub-spoke-landing-zone.observations: Strict retries=0 (1 add-cells batch, 0 clear-diagram, 0 corrective MCP calls). Friction events: (1) 2 search-shapes calls (15+4 queries) — T-035 single-batch contract NOT honored when shape variety is high (~19 distinct icons); (2) XML-extraction round-trips between add-cells and finish-diagram; (3) post-finish format-inspection cycles; (4) NEW: validator-driven coordinate-edit attempts via sed/python — agent saw T-006 overlap warnings and tried to repair coordinates outside MCP rather than re-issuing edit-cells; (5) re-validate cycle after the fix attempt. Cost: 0 + 5 = 5 (vs baseline 4 — +1 regression in cost). BUT rubric mean +0.57: 3.43/4 vs baseline 2.86/4 — first G2 above 3/4 acceptance bar. Source: Copilot Chat session 967060e5-4500-4ab0-b96e-1ab1324c6848.
quality_issues: BREAKTHROUGH (+0.57): rubric mean 3.43/4 — second above-bar capture in the recapture, matching G7-baseline (best of all 7 baseline captures). Layout +1, styling +1, semantics +1, type-fit +1.
WIN (semantics +1): Trust Boundary now rendered as a labeled dashed-red container wrapping On-Premises Network (was missing entirely in baseline). T-019/T-008 working.
WIN (semantics): 3 subscription scopes labeled and rendered as containers per references/semantic-zones.md. Subnet labels visible inside each VNet.
WIN (styling): zero post-export sed correction in the original write — the T-027 apex-arch presets produced palette-correct output. (The sed at [31] was for COORDINATES, not palette.)
WIN (labelling): legend cell at bottom encodes line styles (VNet Peering blue-solid, ExpressRoute Circuit orange-dashed) AND color key for container types (subscription / VNet / subnet types).
WIN (cross-cutting): 'Cross-Cutting Services' row labeled, no edges to/from main flow per abstraction-rules.md.
WIN (type-fit +1): network type with peering edges, gateway subnet, firewall subnet, bastion subnet — all signatures present.

PERSISTING ISSUE (labelling 2/4): paired-shape collisions still visible — 'Web App 1Web App 2', 'Key Vault (PE)Storage (PE)', 'SQL MI Cosmos DB (PL)'. The agent's [28]-[31] coordinate fix attempt didn't resolve them. Same bug family as G2 baseline; T-006 validator catches it; T-025 auto-arrange (deferred) is the proper fix.

NEW PATTERN (cost regression +1): the agent now responds to T-006 validator warnings with mid-flight terminal-based coordinate edits rather than re-issuing MCP edit-cells. This is workflow drift — the validator is firing at the right places, but the agent's response pattern adds friction without fixing the issue. Recommended fix: skill-side reinforcement that says 'on T-006 warning, prefer edit-cells over file edits'.
MINOR: 'Trust Boundary / On-Premises' label at top-left corner truncated/colliding with canvas edge. Subnets render as thin colored bars rather than first-class container groups.

g3-event-driven-microservices.observations: Strict retries=0 (1 add-cells batch, 1 add-cells-to-group, 0 clear-diagram, 0 corrective MCP calls). Friction events: (1) 2 search-shapes calls (8+4 queries — same shape-variety pattern as G2; T-035 single-batch contract NOT honored at high variety); (2) verbose XML round-trips between add-cells / add-cells-to-group / finish-diagram (4 read_file + 5 terminal); (3) post-export palette fix via multi_replace_string_in_file at [42] — T-027 apex-arch presets DID NOT prevent this; (4) re-validate cycle. Cost: 0 + 4 = 4 (vs baseline 3 — +1 regression in cost). BUT rubric mean +0.72: 3.43/4 vs baseline 2.71/4 — LARGEST quality improvement of any recapture so far. First time the agent created zones for a sequence diagram (Ingress / Processing / Persistence containers + Observability zone). Sibling-spacing rule from commit 1885076 not exercised — G3 is vertical-flow, no paired siblings. Source: Copilot Chat session 59a6370d-e717-4ff4-808f-022e072db00c.
quality_issues: BREAKTHROUGH (+0.72): rubric mean 3.43/4 — largest improvement of any recapture (G1=+0.43, G2=+0.57, G3=+0.72). Layout +2, styling +1, semantics +1, labelling +1.

WIN (semantics +1): three logical zones rendered as labeled dashed-border containers per references/diagram-types.md sequence type — Ingress (dashed-red, de facto trust boundary), Processing (blue), Persistence (amber). Observability zone labeled at bottom.
WIN (layout +2): 50% empty canvas issue from baseline largely resolved — diagram fills the upper 40% with content; bottom 60% still under-used but observability zone occupies a chunk.

WIN (semantic correctness): change-feed flow now correct: orders-api -> Cosmos DB AND Service Bus -> orders-worker -> Cosmos -> Event Grid -> notifications-worker. Baseline drew Cosmos -> Redis (wrong) — recapture got the prompt's intent.
WIN (labelling): every edge labelled with protocol + port/auth method per references/diagram-types.md sequence-type rule — HTTPS / OAuth2, AMQP, HTTPS / 443, TLS / 6380, HTTP / webhook.
WIN (type-fit 4/4): legend correctly omitted per OQ-2 sequence carve-out. T-008 validator should pass.

PERSISTING ISSUE (labelling 2/4): two visible label collisions — 'orAMQPipi' (orders-api icon label fused with AMQP edge label running past it) and 'SerAMQPChange Feed' (Service Bus label fused with adjacent edge labels). Different bug class from G2's sibling-icon collisions: these are edge-label-on-icon-label collisions caused by vertical-flow stacking with edge labels routing through.

PERSISTING ISSUE (cost +1): T-027 apex-arch presets did not prevent palette drift on G3. Need to investigate why the agent reverted to non-preset fillColor values. Likely cause: agent crafted custom container styles instead of using get-style-presets output.
MINOR: bottom 60% of canvas still empty after observability zone. Could benefit from a layout that distributes observability across full canvas width.

g4-ml-training-pipeline.observations: Strict retries=0 (1 add-cells batch with 15 cells, 0 clear-diagram, 0 corrective MCP calls). Friction events: (1) 3 search-shapes calls (9+3+3 queries) — same drift pattern as G2/G3, slightly better than baseline G4's 4 calls; (2) XML round-trips (~5 calls between MCP steps); (3) HEAVY validator-driven inspection — 14 read/grep/terminal calls at [27]-[40] trying to formulate a fix for label collisions; (4) multi_replace_string_in_file at [41] for post-export coordinate edits — AGENT IGNORED rule #8 in agent contract about MCP edit-cells over file-level edits (second instance after G2); (5) re-validate cycle. Cost: 0 + 5 = 5 (vs baseline 4 — +1 regression in cost). BUT rubric mean +0.85: 3.14/4 vs baseline 2.29/4 — LARGEST quality improvement of any recapture. 6 of 7 dimensions improved by 1 point each. Variant-aware labels (NC24ads A100 v4, ADLS Gen2 HNS, ACR Premium, gpv2) all correctly applied per references/icon-variants.md — T-018 working. Legend remains best-in-class (two-column Icons + Edges). Source: Copilot Chat session fd479df5-ad4d-4c09-9f30-77a4fcdad009.
quality_issues: BREAKTHROUGH (+0.85): rubric mean 3.14/4 — LARGEST improvement of any recapture (G1=+0.43, G2=+0.57, G3=+0.72, G4=+0.85). 6 of 7 dimensions improved by 1 point each. Confirms the inverted-U complexity hypothesis: medium-complexity scenarios (G3, G4) gain the most from explicit zone/variant cues.

WIN (icon correctness 4/4, +1): variant labels correctly applied per references/icon-variants.md — GPU Cluster (NC24ads A100 v4), ADLS Gen2 (HNS), ACR Premium, Storage Account (gpv2). T-018 + T-032 contract working as designed.
WIN (semantics +1): ML Workspace labeled with RG + region '(rg-mltrain-prod · Sweden Central)' as a zone title. Data Zone properly bordered. T-019 effective.
WIN (type-fit 4/4, +1): deployment-type signatures all present — workspace zone + data zone, variant-bearing icons, AML SDK / ABFS / Pull edge labels.
WIN (legend, best-in-class): two-column Icons + Edges legend retained from baseline G4 with the same high quality. T-021 template aligns with what was already working.

PERSISTING ISSUE (labelling 2/4): 'AMIAML SDKace' triple-label fusion at top of ML Workspace zone — same exact bug class as baseline G4. This is edge-label-on-icon-label collision (the AML SDK edge label routes through the AML Workspace icon's label area). Different bug class from G2's sibling-icon collisions; the new sibling-spacing rule from commit 1885076 does not apply. Needs T-025 auto-arrange or a new edge-label-routing rule.

PERSISTING ISSUE (cost +1): agent used multi_replace_string_in_file at [41] for post-validator coordinate edits despite explicit rule #8 in the agent contract that says 'use MCP edit-cells, not file-level edits'. SECOND instance of agent ignoring this rule (G2 was the first via sed). The contract instruction is not landing under post-validator-warning pressure. Recommend strengthening the rule with concrete consequences (e.g., 'file-level edits create cell-ID drift that breaks subsequent MCP operations').

PERSISTING ISSUE (layout 2/4): bottom 50% canvas still empty after observability/cross-cutting row. Cross-cutting (Application Insights, Log Analytics, Key Vault) better distributed than baseline but still lacks an Observability zone container.

MINOR: ',Pull'/'S' fragment near the GPU Cluster → ADLS Gen2 edge — partial label visible suggesting another edge-label-on-icon collision (the 'Pull' edge label routing past 'GPU Cluster (NC24ads A100 v4)').

g5-enterprise-landing-zone.observations: STRICT RETRY DROPPED FROM 1 TO 0. Baseline G5 was the only scenario to trigger the strict rule (clear-diagram + full rebuild at >20-resource scale). Recapture: 1 add-cells batch, 0 clear-diagram, 0 corrective MCP calls. Strict-rule pattern eliminated at the same complexity level. Tool sequence: 2 setup -> 2 search-shapes (15+5) -> create-groups (7 groups, biggest yet) -> add-cells -> add-cells-to-group -> finish-diagram -> save -> palette fix. Friction events: (1) 2 search-shapes calls; (2) XML round-trips between batches; (3) palette inspection ([28]-[30]); (4) single-call palette fix at [31] — this time a targeted terminal command, not a sed/multi_replace cascade. Cost: 0 + 4 = 4 (vs baseline 6 — FIRST scenario where post-uplift cost is lower than baseline besides G1). Rubric mean +1.43: 3.57/4 vs baseline 2.14/4 — LARGEST improvement of any recapture. 5 of 7 dimensions improved by 2 points each (layout, semantics, labelling, scalability +2; styling, type-fit +1). MG hierarchy now PROPERLY 2-level (was flat in baseline). Source: Copilot Chat session fe9c847b-fc02-4583-8c76-f2a6a58997f3.
quality_issues: BREAKTHROUGH (+1.43): rubric mean 3.57/4 — LARGEST improvement of any recapture. G1=+0.43 G2=+0.57 G3=+0.72 G4=+0.85 G5=+1.43. Confirms inverted-U complexity hypothesis emphatically: the worst baseline scenarios (G4 2.29, G5 2.14) show the largest post-uplift gains.
BREAKTHROUGH: strict retry dropped from 1 to 0. The clear-diagram + full-rebuild pattern that fired only on G5 baseline at >20 resources is gone.
BREAKTHROUGH: cost dropped from 6 to 4 — FIRST scenario where post-uplift cost is meaningfully lower than baseline (G1 also dropped but was already low).

WIN (semantics +2): MG hierarchy now correctly 2-level. Tenant Root MG -> Platform/Landing Zones/Sandbox; Connectivity & Identity rendered as Platform children with tree edges. Baseline collapsed this to one level. T-019 + T-029 zone templates effective.

WIN (semantics +2): 5 distinct labeled subscription scopes (Platform / Connectivity / App Shared / App Workload / Identity). Trust Boundary actually rendered as dashed-red Hub VNet container. Subscription column collisions from baseline ('Connectivity MG hub VNet 10.0.0.0/16' fragment) eliminated.

WIN (scalability +2): the diagram now renders in a logical landscape orientation with clean column-based subscription layout. Baseline failed scalability (1/4) at this resource count; recapture handles it cleanly without decomposition.
WIN (cross-sub edges): VNet Peering edge from Hub to App Workload spoke routes cleanly through the Spoke VNet container. Baseline's edge crossed unrelated subscriptions; recapture is clean.
WIN (no AKS/ACR confusion): two AKS rendered intentionally per prompt (App Shared has AKS Shared; App Workload has AKS Private CNI). Baseline conflated these.

PERSISTING ISSUE (labelling 3/4): 'Connectivity MGtform Subscription Identity MG' at top-left — three Platform-MG-children labels stacked with overlapping positions. Same edge-label-on-icon-label class as G3/G4.
PERSISTING ISSUE: 'Private Link VFN Gateway' in middle of Hub — VPN Gateway label fused with Private Link edge label. Same bug class.
MINOR: Azure Monitor + Azure Policy still floating bottom-left without an observability zone container. Cross-cutting drift partially resolved but not fully.

g6-hyperscale-platform.observations: DECOMPOSITION HELD: 3-page output (Overview / Sweden Central Detail / Germany West Central Detail) per references/large-architecture-decomposition.md. Per-page cells: 27 / 24 / 24 — all within T-023 tier `>50` ceiling of 30. Total 34 image cells (8 + 13 + 13). Tool sequence: 8 reference reads upfront (good — agent loaded full contract) -> 2 search-shapes (15+2) -> 1 add-cells (overview, 23 cells) -> 2 finish-diagram for overview -> per-region: create-groups (3) + add-cells (18) + add-cells-to-group + finish-diagram + save. Friction events: (1) 2 search-shapes calls; (2) overview XML round-trips; (3) TWO finish-diagram for overview ([20]+[29]) — same workflow recovery as baseline G6; (4) XML round-trips for 2 detail pages; (5) T-008 validator-driven trust-boundary inspection ([50]-[55]); (6) T-008 fix + Python multi-page merger. Cost: 0 + 6 = 6 (HELD — same as baseline). The Python multi-page merger remains needed (T-037 deferred). Rubric mean +0.57: 3.71/4 vs baseline 3.14/4 — second-best recapture rubric. 4 of 7 dimensions improved by 1 point each. Trust Boundary now properly rendered (was missing on baseline overview). HTML-entity bug in legend eliminated (T-021 template working). INTERESTING: agent now renders region zones as text-rich containers on overview (with bulleted resource lists inside) and reserves icons for detail pages. New decomposition idiom — works well for high-density >50 scenarios. Source: Copilot Chat session db31bdf9-0289-4dca-9791-1956be089128.
quality_issues: BREAKTHROUGH (+0.57): rubric mean 3.71/4 — second-best of all recaptures. G6 baseline was already above-bar (3.14); recapture pushes it to near-top.
WIN (semantics +1): Trust Boundary rendered as labeled dashed-red container around Front Door + Traffic Manager Profiles on overview page. Baseline had this in legend only. T-019 + T-008 effective.

WIN (labelling +1): legend reads cleanly without 
 HTML-entity bug from baseline. T-021 template working as designed.

WIN (cost held): friction event #6 shifted from 'custom Python multi-page merger' (baseline) to 'T-008 trust-boundary fix' (recapture). The merger is still needed (T-037 deferred), but the validator-driven repair pattern now appears at high complexity — same friction count, different cause.

WIN (decomposition idiom): agent now renders region zones as text-rich containers on the overview (bulleted resource lists inside green dashed containers) and reserves the icon-rich layout for detail pages. New idiom for high-density >50 scenarios; works well.

PERSISTING ISSUE: edge-label stacking near top of overview — 'Front Door routing - Multi-master writes' and 'DNS failover - Geo-pair replication' labels appear concatenated. Same edge-label-on-edge-label collision class as G3/G4/G5; T-006 doesn't catch this (it's about icon-vs-icon), and T-025 auto-arrange (deferred) is the proper fix.
PERSISTING ISSUE: 'Trust Boundary (Public Ingress)' label sits half-outside the boundary container. Minor.

PERSISTING ISSUE: Sentinel + App Insights + Defender + Azure Monitor floating at bottom of overview without observability container. Same drift as G1/G3/G5. T-019 observability-zone snippet exists but agent isn't applying it consistently.

g7-multi-region-active-active.observations: Strict retries=0 (1 add-cells batch, single-batch flow throughout). Tool sequence: 8 setup/reference -> 1 search-shapes (9 queries — T-035 contract HONORED) -> 2 suggest-group-sizing (first scenario to use this MCP tool — agent precomputing container dimensions per the new spacing rules) -> 1 create-groups (5) -> 1 add-cells -> 1 add-cells-to-group -> 1 finish-diagram -> save -> validate -> sibling-overlap fix. Friction events: (1) XML round-trips; (2) 2 suggest-group-sizing precomputation calls — desirable behavior but adds friction; (3) post-validate sibling-overlap analysis; (4) terminal-based sibling-overlap fix at [27] (NOT MCP edit-cells, third instance of agent ignoring rule #8 after G2/G4). Cost: 0 + 4 = 4 (vs baseline 3 — +1). The +1 cost is mostly from suggest-group-sizing calls and validator-driven repair; both signals of better behavior, not regression. Rubric mean +0.57: 4.00/4 vs baseline 3.43/4 — FIRST PERFECT-SCORE recapture. 4 of 7 dimensions improved by 1; 3 already at maximum. Source: Copilot Chat session 6b018efc-fa4e-48f9-ba16-3ce6b7f22e09.
quality_issues: BREAKTHROUGH (+0.57): rubric mean 4.00/4 — FIRST PERFECT-SCORE recapture. All 7 dimensions at maximum. G7 baseline was already best at 3.43; recapture pushes to ceiling.

WIN (semantics +1): Trust Boundary at Front Door rendered as labeled dashed-red container (was missing in baseline). Public Internet zone with Azure DNS Zone inside rendered top-left. Workload — Active sub-zones labeled inside each region.
WIN (layout +1): perfect region symmetry. Web App / Cosmos / App Plan / Storage GZRS / Key Vault rendered identically per region. No empty canvas.

WIN (labelling +1): Cosmos labeled per region writes ('Cosmos DB (SE writes)' ↔ 'Cosmos DB (DE writes)'), Telemetry edge direction fixed (App Insights → Log Analytics, was cross-region in baseline). Inline legend reads cleanly.
WIN (workflow): T-035 single-batch search-shapes contract honored (1 call, 9 queries — only G7 baseline + G7 recapture have hit this).

WIN: agent used suggest-group-sizing MCP tool proactively for the first time in any recapture — precomputing container dimensions per the new spacing rules in references/abstraction-rules.md (commit 1885076).
MINOR: top-of-canvas title is missing (baseline G7 didn't have one either; not a regression). Consider adding 'Multi-Region Active-Active Web Platform' as the diagram name.

MINOR: third instance of agent using terminal-based fix instead of MCP edit-cells (rule #8 in agent contract). G2 used sed, G4 used multi_replace_string_in_file, G7 used terminal at [27]. Pattern is now well-documented; would benefit from an even sharper rule statement in the consolidated fix-up commit.
